author: labor00
text:
## 🐛 Bug

To run the mnist example from <https://github.com/pytorch/examples> is taking more than twice long the usual

## To Reproduce

I have built the pytorch package from the repo ( BUILD\_TORCH=ON python setup.py bdist\_wheel).

I also have modified the num\_workers option of the mnist example script from 1 to 8.

A lot of time is spent on iomp functions (see figure):  
[![perf_flamegraph_hotspot_mnist_master](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36332518/56854438-ea1ffe00-690c-11e9-8cea-3f29b30397b6.jpg)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36332518/56854438-ea1ffe00-690c-11e9-8cea-3f29b30397b6.jpg)

Setting the environment variable OMP\_NUM\_THREADS=1 reduces the time to the usual ones on this machine.

## Expected behavior

To run at the usual speed without needing tho change the OMP\_NUM\_THREADS environment variable.

## Environment

PyTorch version: 1.1.0a0+a25b795  
Is debug build: No  
CUDA used to build PyTorch: 10.1.105

OS: Fedora release 29 (Twenty Nine)

The issue [#18398](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/18398) still occurring. I have copied the relevant libraries to a folder in the LD\_LIBRARY\_PATH.

author: ilia-cher
text: You've modified number of workers from 1 to 8, so I assume you use 8 parallel threads, each of them calls a BLAS function (or explicitly uses OpenMP parallelization). OpenMP creates a new thread pool for each newly created thread, so essentially this resulted in 8 times more OpenMP threads created. You might see this drop in performance because of this, that would also explain why setting OMP\_NUM\_THREADS to 1 helps.

author: labor00
text:
The `num_workers` option affects the number of processes created by the dataloader, the training is performed by the GPU.

The issue here is the change of behavior before and up to 1.0 ( I have tested using the 1.0 binary and it works without performance degradation without setting up the OMP\_NUM\_THREADS=1, but the 1.1 version also presents the behavior described here) this code executed without creating these extra threads.

As pointed out by [@ilia-cher](https://github.com/ilia-cher) this probably are occurring due to the parallelization of some torch functions. However this behavior change can degrade performance and be somewhat of a surprise due to the common use of spawning processes by the the DataLoader, for instance the issue [#19996](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/19996) appears to be a instance of the behavior described here.

author: ilia-cher
text: btw, regarding OpenMP behavior - we're working on improving parallelism handling within core PT, I posted a broad plan in [#19002](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/19002) - soon we'll be able to use parallelism other than OpenMP, e.g. native thread pool or Intel TBB
